LHX.sentence: L3Harris slipped a little today, and the bigger story is a cost problem on one of their programs that's squeezed their profits well below what the other big defense contractors earn — which is why the stock has fallen so much this year, though the underlying demand for defense gear hasn't changed.

ASML.sentence: ASML hit fresh record highs again — the only company on earth that makes a certain kind of chipmaking machine keeps riding the AI wave, though the price is now priced for a lot of optimism.

LIN.sentence: Linde is having a quiet, uneventful stretch — they supply oxygen and other industrial gases under long-term contracts, and that steady business rarely does anything dramatic.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft has fallen surprisingly hard for such a giant company — down more than a quarter from its high — even though the AI cloud business it powers keeps growing, so this looks more like market mood than anything broken.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher dipped a bit, but biotech funding is thawing nicely, and since these labs order their equipment a couple quarters later, that's a good sign for what's coming.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge, the pipeline toll-collector, is holding fairly steady, though its dividend is less generous than usual compared to safe government bonds, meaning it looks a touch expensive here.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow has fallen by half from its peak, which is rare for a company still signing up big corporations and government agencies for its workflow-automation and AI tools.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore drifted down even as copper prices rose, so the miner is lagging the metal it digs up — that gap sometimes closes, but it's been stubborn.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin ticked up a touch, but every time it's dipped this cycle the bounce hasn't followed through, so this quiet stretch is worth watching rather than chasing.

KOF.sentence: The Latin American Coca-Cola bottler barely moved, and since its fortunes mostly ride on the Mexican peso — which is holding steady — there's not much of a story this week.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is having a good stretch, paying a hefty dividend while oil prices sit comfortably above the level it needs to make money, giving it plenty of breathing room.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk, the Danish container-shipping giant that hauls goods across the oceans, slipped slightly and is trading below the value of its own fleet of ships.

MA.sentence: Mastercard drifted down a bit, but the business itself is fine — people keep tapping their cards everywhere, and it's now priced barely above its twin, Visa, unusually close for these two.

ISRG.sentence: The da Vinci surgical robot company had a decent day, and after a big pullback it's now cheap by its own history — hospitals keep using the machines, and each operation sells more of the disposable instruments that drive most of the profit.
